articles.title: 84% OF PRODUCT MANAGERS FEAR THEIR PRODUCTS WILL FAIL
articles.summary: Your product manager friends are stressed. Really stressed. Atlassian surveyed 1,000+ of them - and most are convinced they're heading for failure.

PRODUCT-CENTRICITY WHEN YOU DON'T SELL A DIGITAL PRODUCT
articles.summary: Non-digital companies can't copy tech's product models wholesale. True change means linking teams, strategy, and outcomes across a complex ecosystem, not simplifying it away.

articles.title: CUSTOMER INTERVIEW ANALYSIS: WHERE AI HELPS AND HURTS
articles.summary: Many teams use AI to handle interview synthesis, but this shortcut can erode empathy, context, and research skill. The most effective approach is human-led synthesis supported by AI as a partner - using it to organize notes, surface new insights, and accelerate learning without losing the human depth that turns stories into strategy.

articles.title: MAXIMIZERS VS. FOCUSERS
articles.summary: Organizations thrive by balancing speed with focus. Strategy works best when the Architect builds structure, the Strategist adapts fast, the Opportunist drives discovery, and the Perfectionist safeguards standards.

articles.title: WHAT TO DO WHEN YOUR MANAGER DOESN'T HAVE A STRATEGY
articles.summary: Leaders often have a strategy that they just haven't put into words. The key is to draw it out by asking thoughtful questions, drafting what you've heard, and shaping it into a clear plan they can refine and own.

articles.title: TOO MUCH FEEDBACK, TOO LITTLE CLARITY? HERE'S HOW PRODUCTBOARD'S AI FIXED IT
articles.summary: Product leaders are drowning in scattered feedback, making it hard to see what customers truly need.
